DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Kleczek, Esq. 510-663-7100 of Kleczek Law Ofice for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Teri Crawford, Esq. 650-989-5400 of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley for Defendant Turner.
Ulla Pajala, Esq. 510-444-6800 of Burnham Brown for Defendant California Drywall.
Paul Vaillancourt, Esq. 415-913-4927 of LeClair Ryan for Defendant Hines.
Michael Walker, Esq. 415-362-2580 of Lewis Brisbois Bsgaard & Smith for Defendant Performance Contracting through Court Call.
Sadaf Nejat, Esq. 714-634-2522 of WFBM LLP for Defendant Natioanl Ar Balance Company through Court Call.
Ha eun Cho, Esq. 949-252-0550 of London Fischer LLP for Defendant Marelich Mechanical through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces there are several motions filed but the issues are identical. Plaintiff argues punitive damages. Defendants Attorney in the Courtroon respond.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PERFORMANCE CONTRACTING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on all motions.Defendant Performance Contracting, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The allegations of the first amended complaint disclose that plaintiff was on inquiry notice with regard to her claim at the latest by October 2015. (First Amended Complaint, 33, 39, and 40.) Counsel Kleczek, who represents other plaintiffs with claims similar to plaintiff Rivera's, explains that these allegations only pertained to the other plaintiffs and they have no relevance to plaintiff Rivera's claim. (Kleczek Dec.) The court finds that counsel's drafting error provides a plausible explanation for allowing amendment and the sham pleading doctrine does not apply. (See Smyth v. Berman (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 183, 195.) Plaintiff therefore has leave to amend to allege facts regarding when plaintiff Rivera became injured and/or the mode and manner when she discovered her injury to render her claim timely.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complainance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
